name: Beef Wellington #2
recipeIngredient: Salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Allow the frozen Patty shells to thaw in the refrigerator for 2 hours before use.
2. If the beef tenderloin roast is longer than desired, fold its narrow end under to form a roast about 12 to 13 inches long.
3. Place the roast on a rack in a shallow roasting pan and sprinkle lightly with salt and flour.
4. Bake in a preheated oven at 425°F until a meat thermometer inserted into the thickest part registers 120°F, about 35 to 40 minutes. Allow it to cool sufficiently to handle for about 20 minutes.
5. In a skillet, cook the mushrooms in butter over medium heat until tender, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat and add the goose liver pâté, cognac, and chives.
6. Make a deep slit in the center of the roast, cutting through only one side but not to the opposite side.
7. Sprinkle the inside of the roast with salt and pepper. Spoon the mushroom-pâté mixture into the opening.
8. On a lightly floured surface, arrange the Patty shells in a rectangle, overlapping edges slightly. Press the edges together to seal them. If needed, moisten the edges with a little water.
9. Roll out the Patty shells into a 16x11-inch rectangle. Place the cooled roast in a greased baking dish measuring 15x10x1 inch.
10. Lay the pastry over the roast, tucking under securely. Brush the pastry with the egg yolk and water mixture.
11. Bake at 425°F for an additional 20 minutes or until the pastry is golden brown. Makes 8 servings.
